item.title: U.S. and UK release 10-point roadmap to harmonize stablecoin, tokenized asset, and digital securities rules across both markets.
GENIUS Act July 18 deadline sets the final rulebook determining which stablecoin issuers can operate in the U.S. market.
SEC plans to propose rules on crypto token offerings, broker-dealer custody, and trading venues in July 2026.
Circle receives OCC approval to establish a federally supervised national trust bank for digital asset custody.
Morgan Stanley receives preliminary OCC approval to establish a national digital asset trust bank for custody and staking.
EU MiCA transition period ended July 1; unlicensed exchanges serving EU users must now comply or exit the bloc.
U.S. Senate CLARITY Act advances, proposing to split digital asset market oversight between the SEC and CFTC.
Australia activates Travel Rule for all regulated crypto exchange transfers from July 1, covering both domestic and international flows.
JPMorgan will accept crypto ETFs as collateral for loans, extending institutional financing to digital asset holdings.
DTCC launches July pilot of tokenized securities platform backed by BlackRock, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, and over 50 firms.
Ostium perpetuals exchange loses $18 million in oracle manipulation attack, continuing a wave of DeFi oracle exploits.
SEC targets decades-old rules to allow Wall Street firms to trade blockchain-based securities on regulated venues.
Aptos blockchain patches critical vulnerability that researchers estimated put $70 billion in digital assets at systemic risk.
Stablecoin market supply rises $4 billion following GENIUS Act signing as banks and asset managers begin issuing.
Summer.fi DeFi protocol loses approximately $6 million in exploit detected July 6 via AI-assisted automation attack.
